sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: AVS - Futebol SAD is in poor form with 3 losses and no wins in their last 5 matches, conceding an average of 1.8 goals per game. Casa Pia has mixed results with 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses in their last 5, showing slightly better resilience but struggling to score consistently.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: The last two encounters between AVS and Casa Pia both ended in 1-1 draws, indicating closely contested matches with both teams likely to score. Neither team has managed a win over the other in recent history.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: AVS shows strength in creating big chances (ranked 3rd) and penalty area entries (ranked 3rd), but struggles with conversion (25% big chance conversion rate). Casa Pia's defensive stats are similar to AVS, with both teams conceding frequently, while their attack lacks potency.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Trends
sections.text: AVS's Babatunde Akinsola is in form with 3 goals in the last 5 matches, posing a threat. Casa Pia lacks standout performers recently, and their games often see fewer than 2.5 goals, suggesting a low-scoring affair.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half of the match between AVS - Futebol SAD and Casa Pia saw the away team take a narrow lead with a goal in the 31st minute by Oluwakorede Osundina. Despite AVS holding more ball possession and creating a higher expected goals figure, they failed to convert their chances, missing a big opportunity. Casa Pia, on the other hand, were clinical with their limited opportunities, capitalizing on one of their few clear-cut chances. The home side showed dominance in duels and aerial battles, but disciplinary issues arose with three yellow cards compared to Casa Pia's one.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attacking momentum
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Casa Pia showing early intent, pushing forward with consistent attacking spells in the opening minutes. However, AVS quickly shifted the momentum in their favor around the midway point of the half, sustaining prolonged periods of pressure in the attacking third. This momentum was disrupted as Casa Pia regained control leading up to their goal, showcasing a strong attacking phase. Towards the end of the half, AVS fought back with renewed vigor, creating several threatening moments, but they couldn’t level the score before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player performances
halftimeSections.text: For AVS, Ángel Algobia stood out with a strong midfield presence, while Rafael Barbosa, despite missing a big chance, posed a constant threat in the final third. On the defensive end, Aderllan Santos made several crucial clearances. For Casa Pia, Oluwakorede Osundina was the difference-maker with his well-taken goal, and Jérémy Livolant contributed significantly with an assist. Defensively, Duplexe Tchamba was solid for the away side with interceptions and clearances to keep AVS at bay.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: AVS demonstrated a clear intent to dominate possession and push forward, with frequent entries into the final third and penalty area. However, their finishing let them down, as they couldn’t convert their statistical superiority into goals. Casa Pia adopted a more pragmatic approach, focusing on efficiency in attack and maintaining a compact defensive structure. Their ability to win fewer duels but still score highlights a clinical edge that AVS lacked in the first half. The home side's disciplinary issues may also affect their composure in the second half if not addressed.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Heading into the second half, AVS will likely push hard for an equalizer, leveraging their possession-based style and attacking intent from the latter stages of the first half. They need to be more clinical in front of goal, especially with players like Rafael Barbosa who have shown promise. Casa Pia, on the other hand, will probably look to protect their lead by tightening up defensively while seeking counter-attacking opportunities. Their ability to stay composed under pressure will be key, especially as AVS are expected to come out aggressively.
